parsed_review.summary: CP00-624 is a descriptor-only Risk-A closeout pack (RP20.P05.M02.S01-RP20.P05.M02.S10, 10 units, micro 'Type And Shape Definition') for the Data Room/VDR P05 fixture type-and-shape head. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms it is internally consistent and boundary-clean. Identity/plan binding match across manifest, baseline, closeout-pack-plan.json, registry (DATA_ROOM_VDR_CORE_CP624_*), contract projections (contracts/data-room-vdr-core-contract.json cp624_*), and scripts/validate-rp20-data-room-vdr-core-contract.mjs: range RP20.P05.M02.S01-S10, unit_count=10, upstream CP00-623, next CP00-625 / RP20.P05.M02.S11. Deliverable distribution fixture:6 / claude_review:1 / implementation:3 = 10 is correctly derived from row-title classification (4 base fixtures + primary/secondary golden case -> fixture via FIXTURE_TITLES; 'Review-required case' -> claude_review via CLAUDE_TITLES; 'Denied case'/'Cross-tenant case'/'Missing context case' fall through to implementation). Implementation is descriptor-only: each row carries descriptor_only/runtime_execution:false/raw_payload_included:false; the freezeCp624Result chain plus DATA_ROOM_VDR_CORE_CP624_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION set all writes_*/dispatches_*/executes_*_runtime/emits_hermes_runtime_receipt to false; validators.js enforces ~40 assertions including synthetic_only, no_real_data, no object-storage access, and the authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true). Three tests (model.test.js) plus the validate script cover coverage, descriptor-only flags, contract-projection equality, and pack-to-pack handoff continuity (CP623->CP624->CP625). No secret/real-data patterns found in source. Claude remains read-only (request: read_only, tools Read/Grep/Glob, model claude-opus-4-8, permission_mode dontAsk); Hermes appears only as deterministic descriptor evidence shape (gate H20 label, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, receipt_shape descriptor_only...no_write), not product runtime. CP178+ forbidden_review_evidence list (not_logged_in, zero_byte_output, malformed_json, fenced_json_only, tool_call_shaped_output, missing_raw_output, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, enterprise_trust_claim) is present; readiness/auth preflight passed (exit 0, stdout 1455 bytes, valid JSON) with no invalid review attempts. The pack correctly reports production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false and the 'data_room_vdr_core_p05_fixture_type_shape_head_descriptor_verified' flag is supported by the evidence. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Two P3 notes only: (1) command-evidence/adjudication/construction-inspection/claude-review-result are in sequence-appropriate pending state and the 11 validation commands run in the harness's later final_closeout_validation step, outside read-only scope; (2) changed-file-scope.json lists new-session-handoff.md in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and lists this pack's own review-artifacts dir under unrelated, a minor classification overlap.

findings.title: Closeout validation/adjudication/inspection finalized in later harness steps, outside read-only review scope
findings.details: At this review step (read_only_claude_run_raw_capture, step 3 of the documented hardening_sequence) the pre-review evidence is correctly pending: command-evidence.json has commands:[] with gate_outcome 'pending_pack_evidence_creation' and hermes_gate.status 'pending', construction-inspection.json verdict is PENDING_REVIEW with production_ready:false, adjudication.md is pending, and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-624/claude-review-result.json is pending_review. The 11 validation commands (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, rp05:matter-core:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) execute in the harness's later final_closeout_validation step and cannot be run in this read-only review (Bash is disallowed). This PASS attests to code-level correctness and boundary integrity of the descriptor pack; it does not attest to command execution results.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with the documented hardening_sequence: ensure final_closeout_validation runs all 11 commands green and that adjudication/construction-inspection are regenerated from this review receipt before commit. Do not flip production_ready until those gates pass and human final approval is recorded.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-lists new-session-handoff.md and lists this pack's own review-artifacts dir under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in both untracked_implementation_files (line 177) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 184), and this pack's own review-artifacts directory artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-624/ appears under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 183). The authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs and the manifest worktree_transition_policy.active_pack_required_untracked_files (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-624/) correctly scope the pack, so this is a minor classification overlap in the generated scope artifact, not a content defect. No implementation, boundary, or closeout-correctness impact. The instruction 'do not infer unrelated dirty files are part of this pack' was followed: the genuinely unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/*, matter docs) are excluded from review scope.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the changed-file-scope generator so a pack's own evidence/review-artifacts directories are not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, to avoid downstream-reviewer confusion. No action required for this pack.
